notes: SHIP v1.14.248 — RETIRE THE CRASH-CART DOOR (legacy deletion, John-signed). Crash-Cart Mode was RETIRED by owner decision long ago (PHASE0-CENSUS: 'do NOT build doors for it; physical deletion waits for R1'), but its door was still live: OPS_TABS still carried a `crashcart` key and #ops-tab-strip still rendered a tappable Crash-Cart pill. Surfaced by a knowledge-graph trace of showOpsTab (the highest-betweenness node in the documented architecture) and CONFIRMED against live code before acting. THREE EDITS, one door: (1) OPS_TABS.crashcart key + its launch-card handler REMOVED (:19615; the trailing comma moves onto `manifest` so the object literal stays valid). (2) 'crashcart' dropped from the `_unhomedOps` host-routing test in showOpsTab (:19649) and from its comment — the tab no longer exists, so it cannot be un-homed. (3) The Crash-Cart pill REMOVED from #ops-tab-strip (:14781), replaced by a comment recording why. ⚠️ WHY ALL THREE, NOT JUST THE KEY: showOpsTab opens with `tab = (OPS_TABS[tab]) ? tab : (currentOpsTab || 'sops');`. Deleting the key while leaving the pill would have made the Crash-Cart button fall through that guard and SILENTLY RENDER THE SOPs TAB — a dead-tap with no warning and no toast. That is the exact defect class .223 (deploy dead-card) and .224 (ops-content dead-tap sweep) exist to kill, and it violates hard rule #1 (no silent failures). The key and its only door had to go together. GUARD (post-edit, on the shipped file): OPS_TABS is now 10 keys (blast, sops, portmap, rackmap, optics, burndown, audits, bom, deploy, manifest) — crashcart absent. Reachable doors into the mode: showOpsTab('crashcart') = 0 · data-tab=crashcart = 0 · onclick=crashcart_toggle() = 0. crashcart_toggle() had exactly ONE caller (the OPS_TABS launch card), so with the card and the pill gone the entire mode is now UNREACHABLE. NOT TOUCHED (strict scope): the mode's own implementation — crashcart_toggle / crashcart_exit / crashcart_pickRack / crashcart_render, the #crashcart-layer overlay markup, and the ~100-line .crashcart-* CSS cluster — is now dead but INTACT, left for the LR-2 atomic legacy sweep that already owns it (order dependency; a CSS-brace-balance job, not a drive-by). Deleting it here would have made this a large diff on an already-unverified stack. ⚠️ LEGACY-STRICT BREAK, EXPLICITLY SIGNED BY JOHN 2026-07-14: this is the first ship to touch #ops-tab-strip markup, so ?legacy=1 is NO LONGER byte-identical — the Crash-Cart pill is gone from the legacy strip too. That is intended: the surface is RETIRED in both houses, and leaving a working door to a killed feature under legacy would be the lie. Ship-discipline rule 7 requires an owner signature for exactly this; it was given. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS brace balance unchanged (no CSS touched this ship), CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0), three-stamp lockstep .247 -> .248. BATCH = 4 (.245 .246 .247 .248), cap 6. ⚠️ .245/.246/.247 remain UNVERIFIED. DEVICE-VERIFY (John), one pass covers all four: [.248] BUILD subtab strip -> the Crash-Cart pill is GONE; the remaining pills (SOPs/BOM/Manifest/Port Map/Rack Map/Optics/Power/Burndown/Audits) all still open their correct panes — in particular tap Rack Map and Optics, the pills that flanked the deleted one, and confirm neither dead-taps nor lands on SOPs. Confirm the same under ?legacy=1 (pill gone there too — expected, signed). [.247] SPARKS s1:001 -> q3400-ra rows read SWITCH, and still show the gold hatch + MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN. [.246] s3:176 -> U31/U29/U28 read UNKNOWN in gold, solid + to scale (no hatch); no tray prints 'undefined'. [.245] s3:176 U27 UFM reads SERVER. STILL OWED, JOHN'S RULING: net-6x100g-02 x6 and fs-media-converter-chassis x5 are BOTH media converters (ruled 2026-07-14) but there is NO media-converter type in the app — awaiting the type/colour decision before that ship · DFW02's one empty-model row @c1:001:38 · the HEIGHT of q3400-ra (448 hosts still hatched).
